creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
John, I have gcc9.2 built and running on FreeBSD 12.1-STABLE #22 r353671M.  It might be helpful to clarify a few things with (which preserves anything in your build logs that you'll like to keep private):

uname -pivKU
make -C /usr/ports/lang/gcc9 -VCFLAGS -VLDFLAGS
which ld as
its sometimes helpful to do provide a verbose compile of something trivial, perhaps audio/beep 
cc -v

these are the first things I look for when I have a build problem.  I've just built binutils 2.33.1 and rebuilt gcc9.2.0 on both amd64 and i386.  So there's nothing peculiar, as of today, under /usr/ports/Mk - where I'm occassionally caught out.  :)
PS i386 ran out of memory when build with 
kern.elf32.aslr.stack_gap=0
kern.elf32.aslr.pie_enable=0
kern.elf32.aslr.enable=0
(This is a heads up for Gerald as other things build with ASLR enabled.)
